cards.back: Default on Ethernet; elects DR/BDR; Hello 10s, Dead 40s; multicast 224.0.0.5/6

cards.front: Default on Ethernet; elects DR/BDR; Hello 10s, Dead 40s; multicast 224.0.0.5/6

cards.back: Default on serial / sub-interfaces point-to-point; NO DR/BDR; Hello 10s, Dead 40s

cards.front: Default on serial / sub-interfaces point-to-point; NO DR/BDR; Hello 10s, Dead 40s

cards.back: Non-Broadcast Multi-Access (e.g. Frame Relay full mesh); elects DR/BDR; neighbors statically configured; Hello 30s, Dead 120s

cards.front: Non-Broadcast Multi-Access (e.g. Frame Relay full mesh); elects DR/BDR; neighbors statically configured; Hello 30s, Dead 120s

cards.back: Treats network as collection of P2P links; NO DR/BDR; Hello 30s, Dead 120s; works on partial-mesh

cards.front: Treats network as collection of P2P links; NO DR/BDR; Hello 30s, Dead 120s; works on partial-mesh
Which OSPF network types elect a DR/BDR?
cards.back: Only **broadcast** and **NBMA**. Point-to-point, point-to-multipoint, and loopback do NOT.

cards.front:
OSPF Hello/Dead timers on broadcast and point-to-point: [...] / 40s.
OSPF Hello/Dead timers on NBMA and point-to-multipoint: 30s / 120s.

OSPF Hello/Dead timers on broadcast and point-to-point: [...] / 40s.
OSPF Hello/Dead timers on NBMA and point-to-multipoint: 30s / 120s.

OSPF Hello/Dead timers on broadcast and point-to-point: [...] / 40s.
OSPF Hello/Dead timers on NBMA and point-to-multipoint: 30s / 120s.

OSPF Hello/Dead timers on broadcast and point-to-point: [...] / 40s.
OSPF Hello/Dead timers on NBMA and point-to-multipoint: 30s / 120s.
Two OSPF neighbors won't form adjacency if their [...] don't match.
What command changes OSPF network type on an interface?
Why change a broadcast interface to point-to-point?
cards.back: Skip DR/BDR election → faster convergence, simpler topology on two-router links (e.g. switched Ethernet between just 2 routers).
